title: ThreadForge Thread
description: A thread: the topology of a piece of engineering work, bounded by a human begin node and a human end node. One thread generates one Claude workflow script.

begin.description: Human start node: the engineer provides the inputs.

args.description: Arg sketch: name -> rough type/requirement prose, e.g. { "boardId": "string (required)" }. Not a strict schema; guides the codegen agent's args reads.

intent.description: One-line statement of why this thread is being run.

end.description: Human end node: what the engineer reviews to call this done.

review.description: What 'validated/done' means; guides the workflow's return shape.

agent.description: A single unit of agent work: an intent and an optional named output.

does.description: Intent in prose. May reference args, upstream produces names, or fanout bindings as {name}.

produces.description: Names this activity's output so downstream activities can reference it.

isolationHint.description: Engineer's hint that this agent mutates files; the codegen agent decides whether worktree isolation is warranted.

sequence.description: C-thread: ordered steps with handoffs between them.

parallel.description: P-thread: fixed branches at once, then a barrier. barrierReason is mandatory: if you cannot say why all results are needed together, you do not need a barrier.

barrierReason.description: Why a later step needs ALL branch results at once.

fanout.description: B-thread (orchestrate: per-item work over a list) or F-thread (compare: N independent attempts at the same work, judged).

over.description: Handoff name. orchestrate: the list to iterate. compare: the single input every attempt receives.

as.description: Item binding name available inside body, e.g. "epic".

agents.description: compare mode only: how many independent attempts to fan out.

loop.description: A long-running thread that repeats until done. Both stop conditions are mandatory: an unbounded loop is a vague loop.

stopCondition.description: When the loop is done (goal reached).

noProgressCondition.description: When to give up (no forward progress).

call.description: Thread composition: invoke another cataloged workflow. Compiles to workflow('<name>', args).

args.description: Arg mapping; values may reference in-scope names as {name}.

produces.description: Names the called workflow's return value for downstream handoffs.
